channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Prince and Princess of Wales meet with families of dance class stabbing attack
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Prince William and the Princess of Wales on Thursday carried out their first joint public engagement since the end of Kate’s chemotherapy by meeting the bereaved parents of victims of a stabbing rampage in the seaside town of Southport.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: In Pacific Northwest, 2 toss-up US House races could determine control of narrowly divided Congress
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — In their battle for Congress, national Republicans and Democrats are keenly eyeing the Pacific Northwest, where two of the most competitive U.S. House races in the country are playing out.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 12 rescued from Colorado gold mine tourist site where elevator mishap killed 1 person
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Twelve people were rescued Thursday night after being trapped for about six hours at the bottom of a former Colorado gold mine when an elevator malfunctioned at the tourist site, authorities said. One person died in the accident.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Residents clean up and figure out what's next after Milton
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Florida residents were continuing to repair the damage from Hurricane Milton and figure out what to do next Friday after the storm smashed through coastal communities and tore homes to pieces, flooded streets and spawned a barrage of deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: JULIO CORTEZ, KATE PAYNE and HAVEN DALEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ethel Kennedy, social activist and wife of Robert F. Kennedy, has died
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Ethel Kennedy, the wife of Sen. Robert F. Kennedy, who raised their 11 children after he was assassinated and remained dedicated to social causes and the family’s legacy for decades thereafter, died on Thursday, her family said. She was 96.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and STEVE LeBLANC, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Rare comet visible in the night sky won't return for 80,000 years
item.description: A rare comet is expected to be visible to the naked eye across the Northern Hemisphere for several weeks in October.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KTLA 5

item.title: Hurricane misinformation causing misery for survivors: Abrams
item.description: Radio caller tells Dan Abrams a relative believes that if he accepts hurricane relief from FEMA, the government could take his house.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Milton by the numbers: At least 5 dead, at least 12 tornadoes, 3.4M without power
item.description: Hurricane Milton's explosive formation stirred high anxiety for Florida less than two weeks after Helene raked the state and much of the Southeast. On Thursday, authorities were still assessing the damage, even as Gov. Ron DeSantis said it fell short of “the worst-case scenario.
item.dc:creator: MARY KATHERINE WILDEMAN and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fisher-Price recalls over 2 million infant swings after 5 deaths reported
item.description: A recall has been issued for more than 2 million Fisher-Price infant swings after five deaths were reported.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, NewsNation

item.title: 14-year-old found floating on fence in Tampa floodwaters after Hurricane Milton
item.description: A 14-year-old had to be rescued after Hurricane Milton's floodwaters trapped him on a floating fence in the middle of Tampa.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How to help the recovery from Milton, Helene
item.description: Nexstar, the parent company of this website, is teaming up to help support Red Cross efforts to offer critical resources after Milton.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Over 135 rescued from Tampa assisted living facility amid 'unprecedented' flooding from Milton
item.description: “This is extraordinary to see this type of flooding, especially in this type of area,” said Hillsborough County Sheriff Chad Chronister.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Milton's deadly onslaught ravages Florida after striking in the dead of night and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Milton and Helene leave students, schools reeling
item.description: As Hurricane Milton slams into Florida — and amid Helene's lingering destruction — students across the southeast are facing severe disruptions to their academic year, posing challenges for both educators and children after they have already witnessed devastation in their communities.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Latest: Milton causes widespread power outages and several deaths in Florida
item.description: Hurricane Milton brought powerful winds, storm surge and flooding to much of Florida after making landfall along the Gulf Coast as a Category 3 storm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Although Milton has moved on, at least 8 are dead and millions remain in the dark
item.description: Rescue teams are plucking Florida residents from the flotsam of Hurricane Milton after the storm smashed through coastal communities.
item.dc:creator: Terry Spencer And Kate Payne, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's 'God Bless the USA' Bibles printed in China, records show
item.description: Thousands of copies of former President Trump's "God Bless the USA" Bible were printed in China, according to trade records.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, WGN 9

item.title: Taylor Swift donates $5 million to Hurricane Helene and Milton relief efforts
item.description: Taylor Swift donated $5 million to Feeding America’s disaster relief efforts for those affected by ­­Hurricane Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Trump-Putin ties are back in the spotlight after new book describes calls
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A new book's assertion that former President Donald Trump may have had as many as seven private phone calls with Russian President Vladimir Putin since leaving the White House has refocused attention on their politically fraught relationship and on Trump's sustained dialogue with w
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jenna Fischer of 'The Office' reveals breast cancer diagnosis, recovery
item.description: Jenna Fischer, the actress well-known for her role as Pam on "The Office," revealed her diagnosis and recovery in a post to social media.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, ABC 4

item.title: iPhone update allows users to text for help when there's no cell service. How it works
item.description: With Hurricane Milton barreling toward Florida's Gulf Coast, many Tampa Bay area residents will be faced with widespread and dayslong power outages, which could affect cellphone service, as hundreds saw during Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Melania Trump waves off 'hand-swatting incident': A 'mere misunderstanding'
item.description: Melania Trump is waving off speculation that she didn't want to hold her husband's hand, chalking up images of her seemingly "swatting" away former President Donald Trump to a "mere misunderstanding.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Sally Field shares 'horrific' teenage abortion experience: 'These are the things that women are going through now'
item.description: Sally Field is opening up about her "horrific" experience of being molested while undergoing an abortion in Mexico as a teenager, saying the traumatic event is one of the reasons she's supporting Vice President Harris's White House bid.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Milton is a Category 5. Florida orders evacuations and scrambles to clear Helene's debris
item.description: BELLEAIR BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Florida’s storm-battered Gulf Coast raced against a Category 5 hurricane Monday as workers sprinted to pick up debris left over from Helene two weeks ago and highways were clogged with people fleeing ahead of the storm.
item.dc:creator: HAVEN DALEY and MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal prosecutors quietly dismiss Chasing Horse's long-dormant sex abuse case
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Federal prosecutors in Nevada have quietly dismissed a long-dormant sex abuse case against Nathan Chasing Horse, though the former “Dances with Wolves" actor still faces criminal charges elsewhere. The federal case was tossed Oct.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amid cyberattack, nation's largest water utility pauses billing
item.description: American Water provides services to more than 14 million people across 14 states and on 18 military installations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: When will we 'fall back?' What Utahns have tried to get daylight saving time permanent
item.description: It is almost time to fall back, the first weekend of November marks the end of daylight saving across much of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Mahon, ABC 4

item.title: FEMA administrator continues pushback against false claims as Helene death toll hits 230
item.description: LAKE LURE, N.C. (AP) — The head of the U.S. disaster response agency continued to forcefully push back Monday against false claims and conspiracy theories about her agency’s response to Hurricane Helene as the death toll from the storm continued to climb.
item.dc:creator: JOHN RABY and GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Social Security COLA for 2025 predicted to be lower than 2024
item.description: The Social Security Administration will announce a new cost-of-living adjustment on Oct. 10, 2024, with the increase expected to be smaller than last year.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, Fox 5

item.title: Costco gold bars are selling out as prices for the precious metal break records: survey
item.description: Move over, gas and hot dogs. Costco shoppers are snatching up the store's 1-ounce gold bars despite the market's lofty price for the precious metal.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Biannual National Prescription Drug Take Back Day happening in late October
item.description: The DEA is encouraging the public to remove unneeded medications from their homes through its National Prescription Drug Take Back Day, which takes place on October 26.
item.dc:creator: Jacque Porter, Fox 5

item.title: Chicago mayor names new school board after entire panel resigns amid a fight over district control
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson named members of a new school board on Monday, batting away criticism of the move days after all seven members resigned amid an escalating fight over control of the public schools in the nation’s third-largest city.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: You may be eligible for lawsuit payout if you ate Breyers vanilla ice cream
item.description: A court ruling on the settlement has not yet occurred, with a final approval hearing set for Nov. 21.
item.dc:creator: Alonzo Small, Fox 5

item.title: James Taylor, Luke Combs, Eric Church to take part in Helene relief concert in NC
item.description: "Concert for Carolina," featuring North Carolina natives James Taylor, Luke Combs and Eric Church, will be held at Bank of America Stadium in Charlotte with all proceeds going to Hurricane Helene relief efforts across the Carolina region, organizers announced.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Ullmann, Fox 5

item.title: Tennessee nurse and his dog died trying to save a man from floods driven by Helene
item.description: “I’ve never had anyone risk their life for me,” David Boutin, the man Boone McCrary had set out to rescue said. “From what I hear that was the way he always been. He’s my guardian angel, that’s for sure.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hurricane Helene aftermath poses election hurdles in critical battlegrounds
item.description: Hurricane Helene has thrown up new hurdles for voters and election officials alike in Georgia and North Carolina, threatening disruptions to the voting process in two of the most critical battleground states.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WGN 9

item.title: Dolly Parton, Walmart announce millions in donations to Helene victims
item.description: East Tennessee's own Dolly Parton is expected to offer her assistance to help with the flood recovery efforts in Newport.
item.dc:creator: Hope McAlee, WGN 9
